subject: English
chapter: Poems – A Tiger in the Zoo, The Tiger, The Panther

activities_projects.title: Draw the Tiger
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the tiger’s appearance and surroundings.
steps: Read 'A Tiger in the Zoo'.
Draw the tiger in the zoo or jungle.
Label key features like stripes, paws, tail, and cage or trees.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will observe and represent the tiger visually.

activities_projects.title: Highlight Key Words
activities_projects.objective: Identify important descriptive words in the poems.
steps: Read all three poems carefully.
Highlight words describing the tiger’s emotions and actions.
Discuss meanings with classmates.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand key vocabulary and descriptive language.

activities_projects.title: Simple Emotion Chart
activities_projects.objective: Recognize emotions expressed by the tiger or panther.
steps: Write down emotions from each poem (e.g., fear, anger, longing).
Use a table with columns for each poem.
Share findings in class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will identify feelings and relate to animal experiences.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of emotion identification and clarity of chart.

activities_projects.title: Compare Tiger’s Actions
activities_projects.objective: Observe differences in tiger behavior across poems.
steps: Make a simple table with two columns: 'Tiger in Zoo' and 'The Panther'.
Write points about tiger’s actions and feelings.
Discuss similarities and differences in class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand basic comparison and contrast.

activities_projects.title: Role Play – Tiger’s Day
activities_projects.objective: Express the tiger’s emotions through acting.
steps: Assign roles: Tiger, Zookeeper, Visitors.
Act out a scene showing the tiger in zoo or jungle.
Express emotions clearly through actions or dialogue.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop expression and empathy.
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of role expression.

activities_projects.title: Label the Tiger’s Features
steps: Look at the picture carefully.
Label parts like eyes, stripes, paws, tail.
Write one line describing each feature.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will learn observation skills and physical traits of animals.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of labeling and clarity of description.

activities_projects.title: Poem Recitation
steps: Select a short stanza from any poem.
Recite aloud with clear pronunciation.
Explain the meaning in one or two sentences to the class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will improve oral skills and comprehension.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, pronunciation, and understanding of stanza.

activities_projects.title: Draw Freedom vs Captivity
activities_projects.objective: Visualize contrasts between wild and caged tigers.
steps: Draw the tiger in a cage and a tiger in the jungle.
Show differences in posture, expression, and surroundings.
Label key elements representing freedom and captivity.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand the concept of captivity vs freedom visually.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, clarity, and attention to detail.

activities_projects.title: Simple Discussion – Lessons from Poems
activities_projects.objective: Understand basic themes of freedom, instinct, and human impact.
steps: Write one lesson you learned from each poem.
Discuss in pairs or groups.
Share examples of how humans affect animals in real life.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will connect poem themes to daily life and animal care.

activities_projects.title: Matching Activity – Emotions and Actions
activities_projects.objective: Associate tiger emotions with corresponding actions.
steps: List emotions (anger, longing, fear) from poems.
Write the tiger’s action corresponding to each emotion.
Discuss answers in class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will link textual descriptions to emotions and behavior.
